briefdescription.para: The default value of a boolean truth value (false).
The minimum value of a boolean truth value (false).
The maximum value of a boolean truth value (true).
The default value of an 8-bit signed integer.
The minimum value of an 8-bit signed integer.
The maximum value of an 8-bit signed integer.
The default value of a 16-bit signed integer.
The minimum value of a 16-bit signed integer.
The maximum value of a 16-bit signed integer.
The default value of a 32-bit signed integer.
The minimum value of a 32-bit signed integer.
The maximum value of a 32-bit signed integer.
The default value of an 8-bit unsigned integer.
The minimum value of an 8-bit unsigned integer.
The maximum value of an 8-bit unsigned integer.
The default value of a 16-bit unsigned integer.
The minimum value of a 16-bit unsigned integer.
The maximum value of a 16-bit unsigned integer.
The default value of a 32-bit unsigned integer.
The minimum value of a 32-bit unsigned integer.
The maximum value of a 32-bit unsigned integer.
The default value of a 32-bit IEEE-754 floating-point number.
The minimum value of a 32-bit IEEE-754 floating-point number.
The maximum value of a 32-bit IEEE-754 floating-point number.
The default value of an array of visible characters.
The default value of an array of octets.
The default value of an array of (16-bit) Unicode characters.
The default value of a 48-bit structure representing the absolute time.
The minimum value of a 48-bit structure representing the absolute time.
The maximum value of a 48-bit structure representing the absolute time.
The default value of a 48-bit structure representing a time difference.
The minimum value of a 48-bit structure representing a time difference.
The maximum value of a 48-bit structure representing a time difference.
The default value of an arbitrary large block of data..
The default value of a 24-bit signed integer (encoded as an int32_t).
The minimum value of a 24-bit signed integer (encoded as an int32_t).
The maximum value of a 24-bit signed integer (encoded as an int32_t).
The default value of a 64-bit IEEE-754 floating-point number.
The minimum value of a 64-bit IEEE-754 floating-point number.
The maximum value of a 64-bit IEEE-754 floating-point number.
The default value of a 40-bit signed integer (encoded as an int64_t).
The minimum value of a 40-bit signed integer (encoded as an int64_t).
The maximum value of a 40-bit signed integer (encoded as an int64_t).
The default value of a 48-bit signed integer (encoded as an int64_t).
The minimum value of a 48-bit signed integer (encoded as an int64_t).
The maximum value of a 48-bit signed integer (encoded as an int64_t).
The default value of a 56-bit signed integer (encoded as an int64_t).
The minimum value of a 56-bit signed integer (encoded as an int64_t).
The maximum value of a 56-bit signed integer (encoded as an int64_t).
The default value of a 64-bit signed integer.
The minimum value of a 64-bit signed integer.
The maximum value of a 64-bit signed integer.
The default value of a 24-bit unsigned integer (encoded as a uint32_t).
The minimum value of a 24-bit unsigned integer (encoded as a uint32_t).
The maximum value of a 24-bit unsigned integer (encoded as a uint32_t).
The default value of a 40-bit unsigned integer (encoded as a uint64_t).
The minimum value of a 40-bit unsigned integer (encoded as a uint64_t).
The maximum value of a 40-bit unsigned integer (encoded as a uint64_t).
The default value of a 48-bit unsigned integer (encoded as a uint64_t).
The minimum value of a 48-bit unsigned integer (encoded as a uint64_t).
The maximum value of a 48-bit unsigned integer (encoded as a uint64_t).
The default value of a 56-bit unsigned integer (encoded as a uint64_t).
The minimum value of a 56-bit unsigned integer (encoded as a uint64_t).
The maximum value of a 56-bit unsigned integer (encoded as a uint64_t).
The default value of a 64-bit unsigned integer.
The minimum value of a 64-bit unsigned integer.
The maximum value of a 64-bit unsigned integer.
The default capacity (in bytes) of a statically allocated CANopen array.
The static initializer for co_array.
An empty, statically allocated CANopen array literal with the default capacity.

memberdef.name: CO_VISIBLE_STRING_NC

briefdescription.para: Converts a visible string literal to a CANopen array with a capacity of at least n bytes.
Converts a visible string literal to a CANopen array.

memberdef.name: CO_OCTET_STRING_NC

briefdescription.para: Converts an octet string literal to a CANopen array with a capacity of at least n bytes.
Converts an octet string literal to a CANopen array.
Converts a (16-bit) Unicode string literal to a CANopen array with a capacity of at least n bytes.

memberdef.name: CO_UNICODE_STRING_NC_
memberdef.initializer: (((struct { \ struct co_array_hdr hdr; \ union { \ char16_t us[MAX(ALIGN(n, sizeof(char16_t)), \ sizeof((char16_t[])c)) \ / sizeof(char16_t)]; \ union co_val val; \ } u; \ }){ \ .hdr = { \ .capacity = MAX(ALIGN(n, sizeof(char16_t)), \ sizeof((char16_t[])c)), \ .size = sizeof((char16_t[])c) - sizeof(char16_t) \ }, \ .u = { .us = c } \ }).u.us)

briefdescription.para: Converts a (16-bit) Unicode string literal to a CANopen array.
Converts an array literal with elements of type type to a CANopen array with a capacity of at least n bytes.

memberdef.name: CO_DOMAIN_NC_
memberdef.initializer: (((struct { \ struct co_array_hdr hdr; \ union { \ type dom[MAX(ALIGN(n, sizeof(type)), sizeof((type[])c)) \ / sizeof(type)]; \ union co_val val; \ } u; \ }){ \ .hdr = { \ .capacity = MAX(ALIGN(n, sizeof(type)), \ sizeof((type[])c)), \ .size = sizeof((type[])c) \ }, \ .u = { .dom = c } \ }).u.dom)

briefdescription.para: Converts an array literal with elements of type type to a CANopen array.
Initializes a value of the specified data type to zero.

parameterdescription.para: the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.
the address of the value to be initialized. In the case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Initializes a value of the specified data type with its lower limit.

parameterdescription.para: the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.
the address of the value to be initialized. In the case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer, which will be set to NULL.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Initializes a value of the specified data type with its upper limit.

parameterdescription.para: the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.
the address of the value to be initialized. In the case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer, which will be set to NULL.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Initializes an array of visible characters (CO_DEFTYPE_VISIBLE_STRING).

parameterdescription.para: the address of a pointer. On success, *val points to the first character in the string.
a pointer to the (null-terminated) string with which *val should be initialized (can be NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Initializes an array of visible characters (CO_DEFTYPE_VISIBLE_STRING).

parameterdescription.para: the address of a pointer. On success, *val points to the first character in the string.
a pointer to the string with which *val should be initialized (can be NULL).
the number of characters in the value to be created (excluding the terminating null byte) and the maximum number of characters to copy from vs (unless vs is NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Initializes an array of octets (CO_DEFTYPE_OCTET_STRING).

parameterdescription.para: the address of a pointer. On success, *val points to the first octet in the string.
a pointer to the array of octets with which *val should be initialized (can be NULL).
the number of octets in the value to be created (and the number of octets at os unless os is NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Initializes an array of (16-bit) Unicode characters (CO_DEFTYPE_UNICODE_STRING).

parameterdescription.para: the address of a pointer. On success, *val points to the first character in the string.
a pointer to the (null-terminated) string with which *val should be initialized (can be NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Initializes an array of (16-bit) Unicode characters (CO_DEFTYPE_UNICODE_STRING).

parameterdescription.para: the address of a pointer. On success, *val points to the first character in the string.
a pointer to the string with which *val should be initialized (can be NULL).
the number of (16-bit) Unicode characters in the value to be created (excluding the terminating null bytes) and the maximum number of characters to copy from us (unless us is NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Initializes an arbitrary large block of data (CO_DEFTYPE_DOMAIN).

parameterdescription.para: the address of a pointer. On success, *val points to the first byte.
a pointer to the bytes with which *val should be initialized (can be NULL).
the number of bytes in the value to be created (and the number of bytes at dom unless dom is NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Finalizes a value of the specified data type.

detaileddescription.para: It is safe to invoke this function multiple times on the same value.

parameterdescription.para: the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.
the address of the value to be finalized (can be NULL). In the case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer.

briefdescription.para: Returns the address of the first byte in a value of the specified data type.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the address of the first byte in the array.

parameterdescription.para: the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.
the address of the value (can be NULL). In the case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer.

briefdescription.para: Returns the size (in bytes) of a value of the specified data type.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the number of bytes in the array.

parameterdescription.para: the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.
the address of the value (can be NULL). In the case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer.

memberdef.argsstring: (co_unsigned16_t type, void *val, const void *ptr, size_t n)
memberdef.name: co_val_make

briefdescription.para: Constructs a value of the specified data type.

parameterdescription.para: the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.
the address of the value to be constructed. In the case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer. Note that this value is not finalized before the copy is performed.
a pointer to the bytes to be copied. In case of strings or domains, ptr MUST point to the first byte in the array.
the number of bytes at ptr. In case of strings, n SHOULD exclude the terminating null byte(s).

simplesect.para: the number of bytes copied (i.e., n), or 0 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Copies one value to another.

detaileddescription.para: In case of strings or domains, this function performs a deep copy (i.e, it copies the array).

parameterdescription.para: the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.
a pointer to the destination value. In the case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer. Note that this value is not finalized before the copy is performed.
a pointer to the source value. In the case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes copied (i.e., the result of co_val_sizeof(type, src)), or 0 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Moves one value to another.

detaileddescription.para: In case of strings or domains, this function performs a shallow copy (i.e., it copies the pointer to the array).

parameterdescription.para: the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.
a pointer to the destination value. In the case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer. Note that this value is not finalized before the move is performed.
a pointer to the source value. In the case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer (which is set to NULL on exit).

simplesect.para: the number of bytes copied (i.e., the result of co_type_sizeof(type)), or 0 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Compares two values of the specified data type.

parameterdescription.para: the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.
a pointer to the first value. In case of string or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer.
a pointer to the second value. In case of string or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer.

simplesect.para: an integer greater than, equal to, or less than 0 if *v1 is greater than, equal to, or less than *v2.

memberdef.argsstring: (co_unsigned16_t type, void *val, const uint_least8_t *begin, const uint_least8_t *end)
memberdef.name: co_val_read

briefdescription.para: Reads a value of the specified data type from a memory buffer.

parameterdescription.para: the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.

the address at which to store the value. On success, if val is not NULL, *val contains the read value. On error, *val is left untouched. In the case of strings or domains, val MUST be the address of a pointer. Note that this value is not finalized before the read value is stored.
a pointer to the start of the buffer.
a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer. At most end - begin bytes are read. For strings and domains, all bytes are considered to be part of the value.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes read, or 0 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

memberdef.argsstring: (co_unsigned16_t type, void *val, const void *ptr, size_t n)
memberdef.name: co_val_read_sdo

briefdescription.para: Reads a value of the specified data type from an SDO buffer.

parameterdescription.para: the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.

the address at which to store the value. On success, if val is not NULL, *val contains the read value. On error, *val is left untouched. In the case of strings or domains, val MUST be the address of pointer. Note that this value is not finalized before the read value is stored.
a pointer to the bytes in the SDO.
the number of bytes at ptr.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or an SDO abort code on error.

briefdescription.para: Writes a value of the specified data type to a memory buffer.

parameterdescription.para: the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.
the address of the value to be written. In case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer.
a pointer to the start of the buffer. If begin is NULL, nothing is written.
a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer. If end is not NULL, and the buffer is too small (i.e., end - begin is less than the return value), nothing is written.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes that would have been written had the buffer been sufficiently large, or 0 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: This header file is part of the CANopen library; it contains the CANopen value declarations.

detaileddescription.para:
